findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Log file path traversal in LoggerRequestProcessor
 * @description Log path from network commands reaching file read operations without containment check
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/log-path-traversal-processor
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking

// Find the LoggerRequestProcessor and related log reading methods
from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerRequestProcessor") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "RandomAccessFile") or
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "InputStream") or
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File")
  )
select mc, "File access in LoggerRequestProcessor: " + mc.getMethod().getName()
findings.hypothesis: LoggerRequestProcessor performs file operations based on externally-supplied log paths

message.text: File access in LoggerRequestProcessor: exists
File access in LoggerRequestProcessor: delete
File access in LoggerRequestProcessor: read
File access in LoggerRequestProcessor: exists
File access in LoggerRequestProcessor: isFile

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Log path from network command flows to file operations without validation
 * @description In LoggerRequestProcessor, a path from a deserialized command flows to file read ops
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/log-path-network-to-file
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Source: any getter on command objects that carry log file paths
module LogPathConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("getPath") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("getLogPath") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("getFilePath")
      |
      source.asExpr() = mc and
      mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerRequestProcessor")
    )
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(ClassInstanceExpr ce |
      ce.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
      sink.asExpr() = ce.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("readPartFileContent") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("rollViewLogLines") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("getLogBytes") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("removeTaskLog")
      |
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
  }
}

module LogPathFlow = TaintTracking::Global<LogPathConfig>;
import LogPathFlow::PathGraph

from LogPathFlow::PathNode source, LogPathFlow::PathNode sink
where LogPathFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "Log path flows to file operation from $@.", source.getNode(), "path from network command"
findings.hypothesis: Log path extracted from network command flows to file operations in LoggerRequestProcessor without validation

message.text: Log path flows to file operation from [path from network command](1).
Log path flows to file operation from [path from network command](1).
Log path flows to file operation from [path from network command](1).

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerServiceImpl path traversal - HTTP API to file read
 * @description Log path from HTTP API request flows to file reading without proper validation
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/logger-service-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module LoggerServiceConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      (
        mc.getMethod().hasName("readPartFileContent") or
        mc.getMethod().hasName("rollViewLogLines") or
        mc.getMethod().hasName("getLogBytes") or
        mc.getMethod().hasName("removeTaskLog") or
        mc.getMethod().hasName("queryLog")
      ) and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(ClassInstanceExpr ce |
      ce.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
      sink.asExpr() = ce.getAnArgument() and
      ce.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Logger%")
    )
  }
}

module LoggerServiceFlow = TaintTracking::Global<LoggerServiceConfig>;
import LoggerServiceFlow::PathGraph

from LoggerServiceFlow::PathNode source, LoggerServiceFlow::PathNode sink
where LoggerServiceFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "Log path traversal: user-controlled path from $@ flows to file operation.", source.getNode(), "HTTP request"
findings.hypothesis: HTTP API log path parameter flows to file reading in LoggerServiceImpl without proper path validation

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Path traversal in resource file operations via HTTP
 * @description User-controlled resource paths reaching file I/O in ResourcesServiceImpl
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/resource-service-path-traversal
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.FlowSources

module ResourcePathConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    source instanceof RemoteFlowSource
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(ClassInstanceExpr ce |
      ce.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
      sink.asExpr() = ce.getAnArgument() and
      ce.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Resource%")
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      (
        mc.getMethod().hasName("createOrUpdate") or
        mc.getMethod().hasName("getResourceContent") or
        mc.getMethod().hasName("updateResourceContent") or
        mc.getMethod().hasName("downloadResource")
      ) and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument() and
      mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Resource%")
    )
  }
}

module ResourcePathFlow = TaintTracking::Global<ResourcePathConfig>;
import ResourcePathFlow::PathGraph

from ResourcePathFlow::PathNode source, ResourcePathFlow::PathNode sink
where ResourcePathFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "Resource path traversal: user-controlled value from $@ flows to file operation.", source.getNode(), "HTTP request"
findings.hypothesis: Path traversal in ResourcesServiceImpl where HTTP params reach file operations

message.text: Resource path traversal: user-controlled value from [HTTP request](1) flows to file operation.
Resource path traversal: user-controlled value from [HTTP request](1) flows to file operation.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerServiceImpl methods - full code review for path checks
 * @description Show all statements in LoggerServiceImpl related to path/log handling
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/logger-service-statements
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerServiceImpl") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("queryLog") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getLogBytes") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("rollViewLogLines") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("readPartFileContent") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("removeTaskLog") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getLogContents") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("sendRemoteLog")
  )
select mc, "LoggerServiceImpl." + m.getName() + " calls " + mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Understanding what file operations occur in LoggerServiceImpl and whether paths are validated before use

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerServiceImpl queryLog and getLogBytes - full parameter flow analysis
 * @description Trace the logPath parameter through the queryLog/getLogBytes method chain
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/logger-service-full-flow
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

// Find what parameters are accepted in LoggerServiceImpl public methods
// and what validation (if any) is applied before file reads
from Method m, Parameter p
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerServiceImpl") and
  p.getCallable() = m and
  (p.getType() instanceof TypeString or p.getType().hasName("String"))
select p, "Parameter '" + p.getName() + "' in LoggerServiceImpl." + m.getName() + " - type: " + p.getType().getName()
findings.hypothesis: Understand parameters received by LoggerServiceImpl to trace path injection vectors

message.text: Parameter 'address' in LoggerServiceImpl.getHost - type: String

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerController endpoint parameters and LoggerServiceImpl calls
 * @description Show parameter names accepted by LoggerController endpoints and what methods they call
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/logger-controller-params
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from Method controllerMethod, Parameter p, MethodCall serviceCall
where
  controllerMethod.getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerController") and
  p.getCallable() = controllerMethod and
  serviceCall.getEnclosingCallable() = controllerMethod
select controllerMethod, "Controller method: " + controllerMethod.getName() 
  + " | param: " + p.getName() 
  + " | calls: " + serviceCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + serviceCall.getMethod().getName()
  + " at line " + serviceCall.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Understand exactly what HTTP parameters LoggerController accepts and what service methods they invoke

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name ResourcesController download - HTTP param to file operation 
 * @description Identify all file operations in ResourcesController triggered by HTTP params
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/resources-controller-file-ops
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 *       external/cwe/cwe-073
 */

import java

from Method m, MethodCall mc, Parameter p
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("ResourcesController") and
  p.getCallable() = m and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("downloadResource") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getResourceContent") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("updateResourceContent") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("createOrUpdateResource") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("authorizeResource") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("createDirectory") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("createResource") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("updateResource")
  )
select mc, "ResourcesController." + m.getName() + " - param: " + p.getName() + " - calls: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: ResourcesController HTTP endpoints exposing file operations with user-controlled parameters

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerServiceImpl full method signatures  
 * @description Show all public method signatures in LoggerServiceImpl to identify direct logPath params
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/logger-service-all-methods
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerServiceImpl") and
  m.isPublic() and
  not m.hasName("<init>")
select m, "LoggerServiceImpl." + m.getName() + "(" + 
  concat(Parameter p | p.getCallable() = m | p.getType().getName() + " " + p.getName(), ", ") 
  + ") at line " + m.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Get all public method signatures of LoggerServiceImpl to identify which ones accept logPath string directly

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find all startsWith checks on log/file paths in the entire codebase
 * @description Locate any path containment checks using startsWith to understand what protection exists
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity note
 * @id java/path-startswith-checks
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    m.getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Log%") or
    m.getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Resource%") or
    m.getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%File%") or
    m.getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Path%")
  )
select mc, "startsWith check in " + m.getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + m.getName() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Find any startsWith path validation in Log/Resource/File/Path related classes

    mc.getMethod().hasName("queryTaskInstanceById") or

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerRequestProcessor: path from deserialized network request with no validation
 * @description The log path received from remote Netty command is used directly for file operations
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/logger-request-processor-no-validation
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

// Find what variables in LoggerRequestProcessor are created from deserialized commands
// and flow to file operations without path validation
from Method m, LocalVariableDecl logPathVar, MethodCall fileOp
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerRequestProcessor") and
  logPathVar.getCallable() = m and
  fileOp.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    fileOp.getMethod().hasName("rollViewLogLines") or
    fileOp.getMethod().hasName("readPartFileContent") or
    fileOp.getMethod().hasName("getLogBytes") or
    fileOp.getMethod().hasName("removeTaskLog") or
    (fileOp.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and fileOp.getMethod().hasName("delete"))
  )
select fileOp, "File operation " + fileOp.getMethod().getName() + " in LoggerRequestProcessor." + m.getName() + " at line " + fileOp.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - check if path is validated"
findings.hypothesis: LoggerRequestProcessor performs file operations (delete/read) with no path validation on network-supplied paths

message.text: File operation readPartFileContent in LoggerRequestProcessor.process at line 93 - check if path is validated
File operation delete in LoggerRequestProcessor.process at line 112 - check if path is validated

findings.hypothesis: Path from deserialized network command in LoggerRequestProcessor reaches file reading without any validation

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerRequestProcessor: full taint from RemoveTaskLogRequestCommand path to File.delete
 * @description Path from RemoveTaskLogRequestCommand flows to File.delete - allows arbitrary file deletion
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/logger-request-processor-file-delete
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.DataFlow

// Confirm path deletion in LoggerRequestProcessor without path validation
from MethodCall deleteCall, MethodCall pathGetCall
where
  deleteCall.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasName("LoggerRequestProcessor") and
  deleteCall.getMethod().hasName("delete") and
  deleteCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") and
  pathGetCall.getEnclosingCallable() = deleteCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
  (
    pathGetCall.getMethod().hasName("getLogPath") or
    pathGetCall.getMethod().hasName("getPath") or
    pathGetCall.getMethod().hasName("getFilePath")
  )
select deleteCall, "File.delete() in LoggerRequestProcessor at line " + deleteCall.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - path obtained from: " + pathGetCall.getMethod().getName() + " at line " + pathGetCall.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: File deletion in LoggerRequestProcessor uses network-supplied path without validation - arbitrary file deletion

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name LoggerServer port binding - confirm network exposure
 * @description Identify the port the LoggerServer binds to, confirming network-level exposure
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/logger-server-port-binding
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

// Find port binding and NettyRemotingServer creation in LoggerServer
from ClassInstanceExpr ce, Class c
where
  c.hasName("LoggerServer") and
  ce.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType() = c
select ce, "LoggerServer creates: " + ce.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().getName() + " at line " + ce.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: LoggerServer binds to a network port via Netty, confirming LoggerRequestProcessor is network-accessible
